reforms.description: The government plans to increase spending on health and education by 0.9% and 0.4% of GDP, respectively, to improve human capital and potential growth.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investing in health and education enhances human capital, which is crucial for long-term economic growth and productivity improvements.
reforms.status_evidence: Planned increases in health and education spending are important to improve human capital.
reforms.importance_rationale: The increase in spending on health and education is significant but part of ongoing efforts to enhance human capital rather than a complete overhaul of the system.
reforms.source_quote: In the 2024 budget, spending for health and education will increase by 0.9% and 0.4% of GDP, respectively.

reforms.description: The government aims to raise investments in the green transition to improve energy security and ensure policy continuity independent of EU funding cycles.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investing in the green transition fosters innovation in sustainable technologies and improves energy security, which can enhance economic resilience.
reforms.status_evidence: Raising investments in the green transition, which would improve energy security, is a key focus.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative is significant for promoting sustainable development but is part of broader efforts rather than a standalone major reform.
reforms.source_quote: Raising investments in the green transition, which would improve energy security, is a key focus.

reforms.description: To strengthen innovation and investment demand of firms, it is crucial to lower informality, raise competition and business dynamism, and address skilled labour shortages by investing more in human capital and facilitating skilled migration.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investing in human capital and addressing skilled labour shortages will enhance productivity and innovation capacity in firms.
reforms.status_evidence: The need to strengthen innovation and investment demand was highlighted as a key policy priority.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses systemic issues affecting innovation and investment, aiming for a comprehensive approach to enhance economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: To strengthen innovation and investment demand of firms, it is crucial to lower informality, raise competition and business dynamism, and address skilled labour shortages.

reforms.description: The Bank of Latvia launched the Innovation Hub in 2023, providing free expert advice to FinTech companies on compliance with licensing, supervision, and regulatory requirements.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating compliance and regulatory understanding, the Innovation Hub aims to enhance the growth and sustainability of FinTech firms, thereby supporting innovation in the financial sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The Innovation Hub was officially launched in 2023.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of the Innovation Hub represents a significant step in supporting the FinTech sector, which is crucial for enhancing innovation and competition in the financial market.
reforms.source_quote: The Bank of Latvia has launched the Innovation Hub in 2023, which provides free expert advice to FinTech companies on compliance with licensing, supervision and regulatory requirements.

reforms.description: Latvia has introduced an electronic procurement platform that is mandatory for all levels of government for tenders above certain thresholds, improving transparency and reducing barriers for smaller firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhances competition and reduces costs for public contracts, benefiting both the public sector and innovative smaller firms.
reforms.status_evidence: The use of the electronic procurement platform is now mandatory for relevant tenders.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant overhaul of procurement processes, enhancing efficiency and transparency across the public sector.
reforms.source_quote: Latvia has considerably improved procurement procedures and their transparency by introducing an electronic procurement platform.

reforms.description: Latvia's government plans to spend 42% of the EU Recovery and Resilience Fund to support climate objectives, including investments to overhaul the Riga Metropolitan area transport system and incentivise clean transport and sustainable mobility.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investments in clean transport and sustainable mobility are expected to reduce emissions and improve overall economic efficiency.
reforms.status_evidence: The plan supports the green transition with investments to overhaul the Riga Metropolitan area transport system.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant investment in sustainable infrastructure, which is crucial for reducing emissions and enhancing economic resilience.
reforms.source_quote: Latvia plans to spend 42% of the EU RRF fund to support climate objectives, including investments to overhaul the Riga Metropolitan area transport system.

reforms.description: The government is providing grants and loan guarantees through ALTUM to cover up to 49% of the cost of renovating around 170 buildings as part of the EU Recovery and Resilience Fund.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving energy efficiency in buildings, this initiative is expected to lower energy costs and reduce emissions, contributing to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: ALTUM provides grants and loan guarantees with a budget of EUR 57.2 million to cover up to 49% of the cost of renovating around 170 buildings.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it addresses energy efficiency in the residential sector, which is crucial for reducing emissions and improving living conditions.
reforms.source_quote: As part of the EU RRF funds, ALTUM provides grants and loan guarantees with a budget of EUR 57.2 million to cover up to 49% of the cost of renovating around 170 buildings.

reforms.description: Latvia is strengthening the innovative capacity of the public sector by enhancing resources and investigative powers of the Competition Council, which aims to improve competitive neutrality and foster business dynamism and innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing the Competition Council's capabilities is expected to lower barriers to entry and improve market conditions, which can stimulate innovation and investment.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative to strengthen the Competition Council has been officially announced.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change aimed at enhancing the public sector's role in fostering innovation, which is crucial for economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: Latvia is strengthening the innovative capacity of the public sector by enhancing resources and investigative powers of the Competition Council.

reforms.description: The Latvian government proposed amendments to the Law on Consumer Protection and the Law on Credit Institutions to facilitate easier switching between banks by capping refinancing fees and streamlining the refinancing process.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing competition in the banking sector is expected to lower lending rates and improve access to credit, thereby supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has proposed amendments to facilitate bank switching.
reforms.importance_rationale: The proposed amendments represent a significant step towards improving competition in the banking sector, which is crucial for enhancing credit availability.
reforms.source_quote: The government has proposed amendments to the Law on Consumer Protection and the Law on Credit Institutions, to make it easier for customers to switch banks.

reforms.description: Amendments to the Consumer Rights Protection Law were adopted, introducing a levy on banks to provide mortgage interest rebates to borrowers, aimed at reducing the cost of borrowing.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: While the rebate aims to reduce borrowing costs, it may lead to higher fees elsewhere, complicating its overall impact on the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The parliament adopted amendments to the Consumer Rights Protection Law.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of mortgage interest rebates is a significant policy change aimed at improving affordability for borrowers, though its effectiveness may vary.
reforms.source_quote: In December, the parliament adopted amendments to the Consumer Rights Protection Law, which introduced a levy on banks to provide mortgage interest rebates to borrowers.

reforms.description: The government has set medium-term policy priorities for capital market deepening until 2027, which includes facilitating the listing of SMEs on the stock market and conducting Initial Public Offerings (IPOs) for large state-owned enterprises.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing access to finance for SMEs and improving competition in the financial system can stimulate innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government set medium-term policy priorities for capital market deepening until 2027.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a structural change aimed at improving the financial ecosystem for SMEs, which is crucial for fostering innovation and economic resilience.
reforms.source_quote: In May 2023, the government set medium-term policy priorities for capital market deepening until 2027, which includes facilitating the listing of SMEs on the stock market.

reforms.description: The Ministry of Economy is planning a support programme to cover part of the costs of listing for SMEs on the Nasdaq Baltic stock exchange, aiming to reduce barriers for small firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By lowering the financial barriers for SMEs to access capital markets, this programme can enhance their growth potential and encourage innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The Ministry of Economy is planning a support programme to cover part of the costs of listing for SMEs.
reforms.importance_rationale: This support programme is a significant step towards facilitating SME access to capital markets, which is essential for their growth and innovation capacity.
reforms.source_quote: The Ministry of Economy is planning a support programme to cover part of the costs of listing for SMEs.

reforms.description: The Latvian government plans to allocate an additional EUR 400,000 annually in the 2024-26 budgets to support international exhibitions, investment advice for early-stage start-ups, and sessions aimed at attracting investors.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This funding is expected to enhance the visibility and attractiveness of Latvian start-ups, thereby facilitating access to venture capital and fostering innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has announced plans for additional funding to support start-ups.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant investment in the start-up ecosystem, which is crucial for enhancing innovation and economic growth in Latvia.
reforms.source_quote: Additional annual funding of EUR 400,000 is planned in the 2024-26 budgets to finance international exhibitions, investment advice for early-stage start-ups and sessions to attract investors.

reforms.description: The introduction of a start-up visa scheme aims to facilitate skilled migration, thereby attracting talent to support the growth of innovative firms in Latvia.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By attracting skilled individuals, the scheme is expected to enhance the human capital available for innovation and entrepreneurship in Latvia.
reforms.status_evidence: The start-up visa scheme has been officially announced as part of the government's initiative to support start-ups.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is a significant step towards enhancing the talent pool for start-ups, which is essential for fostering innovation and economic development.
reforms.source_quote: The start-up visa scheme to facilitate skilled migration is a step in the right direction to attract venture capital funds.

reforms.description: ALTUM plans to offer loans with a capital discount for medium or large companies to foster large-scale investments in technology-intensive sectors, contingent on meeting specific performance criteria monitored by the LIAA.
reforms.component_name: Capital Discount Loans for Technology Investments
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encourages investment in technology-intensive sectors, which can enhance productivity and innovation in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: ALTUM plans to offer loans with a capital discount for medium or large companies.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant step towards enhancing investment in technology sectors, which is crucial for economic growth and innovation.
reforms.source_quote: ALTUM also plans to offer loans with a capital discount for medium or large companies to foster large-scale investments in technology-intensive sectors.

reforms.description: The establishment of the Baltic SME IPO Fund aims to support initial public offerings or private placements for Baltic SMEs, enhancing their visibility and potentially attracting venture capital funds.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitates access to capital markets for SMEs, which can lead to increased investment and growth opportunities.
reforms.status_evidence: In June 2023, the Latvian government approved the establishment of the Baltic SME IPO Fund.
reforms.importance_rationale: This fund represents a significant effort to enhance the capital market for SMEs, which is vital for their growth and innovation.
reforms.source_quote: In June 2023, the Latvian government approved the establishment of the Baltic SME IPO Fund, co-financed by ALTUM.

reforms.description: Latvia is developing the Individual Learning Accounts as part of its Recovery and Resilience Facility plan to facilitate access to online learning opportunities aimed at improving adult skills, particularly in digital areas.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing adult skills, especially in digital technologies, is expected to improve workforce productivity and innovation capacity.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative is part of the Recovery and Resilience Facility plan.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative addresses a critical gap in adult digital skills, which is essential for improving overall productivity and innovation in the economy.
reforms.source_quote: Latvia is developing the Individual Learning Accounts, an EU initiative that aims to facilitate access to online learning opportunities to improve adult skills.

reforms.description: The government plans to provide grants of EUR 30 million to improve the digital skills of employees in over 4,200 firms, covering up to 70% of training costs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing the digital skills of employees, the initiative aims to boost productivity and innovation within firms.
reforms.status_evidence: The grants will support programmes to improve basic digital skills.
reforms.importance_rationale: This grant programme is a significant step towards enhancing workforce skills, which is crucial for fostering innovation in the economy.
reforms.source_quote: The Ministry of Economy will provide grants of EUR 30 million covering up to 70% of the total training costs, to improve the digital skills of the employees of more than 4,200 entrepreneurs.

reforms.description: The government plans to consolidate universities and create specialised research clusters to improve cooperation and coordination with the private sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing collaboration between universities and the private sector, the initiative aims to foster innovation and improve knowledge transfer.
reforms.status_evidence: Plans to consolidate universities and create specialised research clusters are welcome.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant for improving the efficiency and effectiveness of research and innovation activities in Latvia.
reforms.source_quote: Plans to consolidate universities and create specialised research clusters are welcome, as the low number of researchers is dispersed across a relatively large number of universities.

reforms.description: Latvia plans to establish a tri-partite training fund to enhance cooperation among firms and training providers, addressing the lack of digital and management skills that hinder innovation and digital adoption.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving skills in the workforce is expected to enhance productivity and innovation capacity in firms.
reforms.status_evidence: The establishment of the tri-partite training fund has been publicly announced.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses a critical gap in skills that affects innovation, with potential significant impacts on firm performance and competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: A lack of digital and management skills holds back digital adoption and innovation.

reforms.description: The governance of innovation support programmes in Latvia will be centralized in a single agency to reduce administrative burdens and improve the evaluation of existing programmes.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Centralizing governance is expected to streamline processes and enhance the effectiveness of innovation support, leading to better outcomes for firms.
reforms.status_evidence: The plan to centralize innovation support has been officially announced.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in how innovation support is managed, potentially leading to more coherent and impactful policies.
reforms.source_quote: Governance of innovation support programmes is weak and the administrative burden is high.
